title: Customize Course Notifications in GoHighLevel — Client Portal
description: Learn how to customize course notifications in GoHighLevel's Client Portal. Step-by-step guide to managing student communications and boosting engagement.
html_content:
Managing client communications at scale is one of the biggest headaches for digital agencies. When you're running courses through GoHighLevel, your students need the right information at the right time—but bombarding them with notifications kills engagement. That's where course notification customization comes in.

GoHighLevel's Client Portal now lets you control exactly which course-related notifications your students receive, when they receive them, and through which channels. This feature transforms how agencies deliver online education while maintaining the personalized touch that keeps clients happy. In this guide, I'll walk you through setting up customized course notifications, why it matters for your business, and how to implement best practices that actually drive engagement.

Ready to take your client portal to the next level? Start your free 30-day GoHighLevel trial today and see how this feature transforms your course delivery.

📋 In This Guide

  • What Are Course Notification Preferences in GoHighLevel?
  • How to Access and Configure Course Notification Settings
  • Key Notification Types You Can Customize
  • Best Practices for Managing Client Portal Notifications
  • Why This Feature Matters for Digital Marketing Agencies
  • Common Notification Mistakes and How to Avoid Them

What Are Course Notification Preferences in GoHighLevel?

Course notification preferences are customizable settings that allow client portal owners and administrators to control how and when course-related communications are delivered to students. Rather than using a one-size-fits-all approach, GoHighLevel now lets you tailor the notification experience based on your course structure, audience needs, and business goals.

This feature represents a significant shift in how digital agencies manage online education. Instead of overwhelming students with automated notifications, you can be surgical about what triggers a message, who receives it, and whether it goes via email, in-app alert, or SMS. The result? Higher engagement rates, fewer unsubscribes, and better student outcomes.

For agencies running multiple courses for different clients, this level of control is essential. You can maintain brand consistency while giving each client the ability to customize notifications for their specific audience.

How to Access and Configure Course Notification Settings

Setting up course notification preferences in GoHighLevel is straightforward, but there are a few key steps to get right.

Step 1: Navigate to the Client Portal Settings
Start by logging into your GoHighLevel account as an administrator. Head to your Client Portal section—this is typically found under the main navigation menu. You'll want admin-level access to modify these settings.

Step 2: Locate the Notification Preferences Section
Within the Client Portal settings, look for "Notification Preferences" or "Course Notifications." GoHighLevel has made this easily accessible, grouping all notification-related controls in one area for simplicity.

Step 3: Select the Course You Want to Configure
If you're running multiple courses, select which course (or courses) you want to customize notifications for. You can set different preferences for different courses based on their content and audience.

Step 4: Toggle Notification Types On or Off
For each course, you'll see a list of notification events. You can enable or disable notifications for specific triggers. This is where precision matters—only activate notifications that truly benefit your students.

Step 5: Choose Delivery Channels
Decide whether notifications should be sent via email, in-app alerts, SMS, or a combination. Consider your audience's preferences and communication habits.

Step 6: Set Frequency and Timing Rules
Configure how often notifications are sent and during which hours. Some agencies batch notifications into daily digests to reduce alert fatigue.

Step 7: Save and Test
Always test your notification settings with a test account before rolling them out to all students. Send yourself a test notification to confirm the message, delivery method, and timing work as expected.

Key Notification Types You Can Customize

GoHighLevel allows you to customize notifications for various course-related events. Understanding which notifications are available helps you create a strategic communication plan.

Course Enrollment Notifications
When a student enrolls in a course, you can choose to send them a welcome message. This is typically high-value because it confirms enrollment and gets them excited about the course.

Lesson Release Notifications
If you release lessons on a schedule, notify students when new content is available. This encourages consistent engagement and prevents students from falling behind.

Course Completion Notifications
When a student finishes a course, a congratulatory notification reinforces the achievement and keeps them in your ecosystem for upsells or additional courses.

Assignment and Quiz Reminders
Remind students about pending assignments or quizzes they haven't completed. These are critical engagement drivers because they prompt action.

Comment and Discussion Notifications
If your course has community features, notify students about comments or responses to their posts. This builds engagement within the course community.

Course Update Notifications
When you update course content, materials, or schedules, notify students so they stay informed about important changes.

💡 Pro Tip

Don't enable every notification type. More notifications don't equal better engagement. Be selective—enable only the notifications that drive meaningful student actions. Track which notification types generate the highest engagement rates and double down on those.

Best Practices for Managing Client Portal Notifications

Personalize Based on Student Behavior
GoHighLevel allows you to set different notification preferences for different student segments. Use this to your advantage. New students might benefit from more frequent reminders, while engaged students might prefer fewer notifications.

Respect Quiet Hours
Configure your notification settings to respect time zones and avoid sending messages at 3 AM. Students who receive notifications at inconvenient times are more likely to unsubscribe.

Use Clear, Action-Oriented Language
Notification content matters as much as frequency. Write notification copy that's clear about what the student needs to do and why it matters.

Monitor Unsubscribe Rates
If you notice students opting out of notifications, it's a sign you're over-communicating. GoHighLevel provides analytics on notification engagement—use this data to refine your strategy.

Create Notification Preference Controls for Students
Give students control over their own notification preferences. This reduces unsubscribes and improves satisfaction because students feel heard.

Why This Feature Matters for Digital Marketing Agencies

For digital marketing agencies offering courses as part of their service suite, course notification customization is a game-changer. Here's why:

Improved Course Completion Rates
Students who receive timely, relevant notifications are more likely to complete courses. Better completion rates mean better outcomes for your clients, stronger testimonials, and more referrals.

Reduced Support Burden
When students know exactly when lessons are released and what's expected of them, they ask fewer questions. This frees your support team to focus on high-value interactions.

Enhanced Brand Credibility
Courses that communicate professionally and strategically reflect well on your agency's brand. It shows you care about the student experience.

Scalability Without Burnout
Automated, customized notifications let you scale course delivery without manually managing every student interaction. This is critical when you're running courses for multiple clients.

Data-Driven Decision Making
GoHighLevel tracks notification metrics. You can see which types of notifications drive the most engagement and optimize accordingly.

Common Notification Mistakes and How to Avoid Them

Mistake #1: Sending Too Many Notifications
The biggest error agencies make is enabling every notification type and setting frequency too high. This causes notification fatigue and high unsubscribe rates. Solution: Start conservative. Enable only essential notifications and increase based on engagement data.

Mistake #2: Using Generic Messaging
Generic notification copy feels impersonal and gets ignored. Solution: Customize notification messages to include the student's name, course title, and specific action items.

Mistake #3: Ignoring Time Zones
Sending notifications at the same time for all students ignores time zone differences. Solution: Use GoHighLevel's scheduling features to send notifications during optimal hours for each student's location.

Mistake #4: Not Testing Before Launch
Launching notifications without testing can result in broken links, formatting issues, or messages sent to the wrong audience. Solution: Always run test notifications through your own account first.

Mistake #5: Never Reviewing Performance
Set notifications and forget them. This is a missed opportunity. Solution: Review notification engagement metrics weekly. Adjust based on what's working.

Final Thoughts

Customizing course notifications in GoHighLevel is one of the most underrated features for agencies managing online education. It's not flashy, but it directly impacts student engagement, completion rates, and your reputation. By implementing the strategies in this guide—being selective with notifications, personalizing messages, respecting student preferences, and monitoring performance—you'll create a communication experience that keeps students engaged and satisfied.
